summarization.a: The figure shows the JV characteristics of devices using TGO as ESL with varying doping values.
summarization.b: The figure shows the JV characteristics of devices using ZGO as ESL with varying doping values.

b.question: How did the Ge content affect the Voc of ZGO based solar cells?
b.answer: As the Ge content was increased for the ZGO films, the solar cells showed a decrease in Voc, compared to solar cells using ZnO . When the Ge content increased further, there was an increase in Voc up to ZGO 0.37.

b.question: How is the performance of CdS based device in both cases?
b.answer: From figure a it appears, the device characteristics of CdS is better than device b as the Voc seems higher in case a than case b.

a.question: What are the device structures used for solar cell characteristics?
a.answer:
1. ZnO:Al/ZnO/TGO/ACIGS/Mo/SLG solar cell stacks
, 2. ZnO:Al/ZnO/ZGO/ACIGS/Mo/SLG solar cell stacks

a.question: How did the Ge content influence the performance of Ge doped tin oxide in the solar cells?
a.answer: Until TGO 0.10, the Voc of the solar cells increased with increasing Ge content. At the same time, the short circuit current density (Jsc) remained unchanged. For TGO 0.16, both the Voc and the FF started to drop off due to a kink in the J−V curve around Voc. Finally, the solar cells with TGO 0.30 show low FF, low Voc and a tiny Jsc, suggesting that the transport of photoexcited electrons has been almost completely hampered.
